OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO COMPEL JUDICIAL REFEREE (TRANSACTION ID # 64073566) FILED BY DEFENDANT FOLSOM VENTURES, LLC LENNAR HOMES OF CALIFORNIA, INC.

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED ON PLAINTIFF 631 FOLSOM STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL THIS ACTION TO JUDICIAL REFERENCE, AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF 631 FOLSOM STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL THIS ACTION TO JUDICIAL REFERENCE IS GRANTED. THE COURT ORDERS LITIGATION OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT STAYED PENDING RESOLUTION OF THE COMPLAINT. THE PARTIES MAY SEEK TO LIFT THE STAY IF THE MANUFACTURERS AGREE TO REFERENCE OR ONCE THE COMPLAINT IS RESOLVED. THE COURT CONTINUES THIS MOTION TO SEPTEMBER 3, 2019. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE APPOINTMENT OF A REFEREE/S. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT AGREE ON A REFEREE, THEN EACH PARTY SHALL SUBMIT UP TO THREE NOMINEES FOR APPOINTMENT BY AUGUST 29, 2019. THE COURT SIGNED THE ORDER SUBMITTED BY EMAIL (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR COMPLETE DETAILS). JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel This Action To Judicial Reference
Off Calendar Sep-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. JOHN J. STANDER, ESQ. of Fenton Grant Mayfield Kaneda & Litt, LLP (Tel: 925-357-3135) appearing for plaintiff 631 Folsom Street Owners Assocation;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF 631 FOLSOM STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Compel This Action To Judicial Reference.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear at hearing regarding appointment of referee and status of judicial reference. Plaintiff's counsel informs the Court that defense counsel has proposed a name to be the judicial referee. The Court takes the matter under submission regarding the appointment of referee and status of judicial reference to locate defense counsel's proposal and to consider the parties' submitted proposals.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel This Action To Judicial Reference
Off Calendar Aug-20-2019 Continued To Sep-03-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted on PLAINTIFF 631 FOLSOM STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Compel This Action To Judicial Reference, as follows:

Plaintiff 631 Folsom Street Owners Association's motion to compel this action to judicial reference is granted. Section 9.1(f) of the parties' CC&Rs, which defendants prepared and executed, expressly states that a judicial referee will resolve the parties' construction defect dispute. (Stander Dec., Paras. 3-6.) This provision is binding on the parties. (See Pinnacle Museum Tower Assn. v. Pinnacle Market Development (US), LLC (2016) 55 Cal.4th 223, 246 [developer could enforce covenant to arbitrate in CC&Rs against homeowners association].) The court does not believe that denial of the motion is necessary to avoid conflicting rulings or prevent judicial waste. (See Tarrant Bell Property, LLC v. Superior Court (2011) 51 Cal.4th 538.) Defendants' cross-complaint for equitable indemnity and contribution and declaratory relief against the manufacturers can be severed and adjudicated after plaintiff's construction defect complaint is resolved. Alternatively, the manufacturer cross-defendants may agree to reference.

The court orders litigation of the cross-complaint stayed pending resolution of the complaint. The parties may seek to lift the stay if the manufacturers agree to reference or once the complaint is resolved. The court continues this motion to September 3, 2019. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding the appointment of a referee/s. If the parties cannot agree on a referee, then each party shall submit up to three nominees for appointment by August 29, 2019.

The court signed the order submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-31-2019 Continued To Oct-02-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FOLSOM VENTURES, LLC, LENNAR HOMES OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

Defendants Folsom Venture, LLC and Lennar Homes of California, Inc.'s demurrer to the complaint is overruled. McMillin Albany LLC v. Superior Court (2018) 4 Cal.5th 241 does not bar the breach of express warranty, breach of implied warranty, and violation of CC&Rs causes of action. All of those claims sound in contract and therefore they are viable. (See Civ. Code 943(a) ["[T]his title does not apply to any action by a claimant to enforce a contract'"]; McMillan Albany LLC, 4 Cal.5th at 249 ["the statute here leaves the common law undisturbed in some areas, expressly preserving actions for breach of contract, fraud, and personal injury. ( 943, subd. (a).)."]; id. at 259 ["Nor does our holding embrace claims such as those for breach of contract'"].) The court concludes that the breach of implied warranty claim sounds in contract in this case. "A warranty is a contractual term concerning some aspect of the sale'" (Windham v. Superior Court (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th1162, 1168; see also Freas v. BMW of North America, LLC (2018) 320 F.Supp.3d 1126, 1132 ["Under California law, a claim for breach of express or implied warranties 'necessarily sounds in contract.'"]; Allstate Ins. Co. v. Loo (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 1794, 1798 ["Allstate did assert a contractual cause of action: breach of the implied warranty of habitability."].)

The court further notes that plaintiff is seeking purely economic and property damages. "The precise nature of the claim is ordinarily identified by examining the damages alleged: when the damages are purely economic, the claim sounds in contract, but a ... claim alleging damages for death or personal injury sounds in tort." (Rodriguez v. Christus Spohn Health System Corp. (5th Cir. 2010) 628 F.3d 731, 736.)

Lennar Homes of California, Inc. contends that it lacks privity with plaintiff on the CC&Rs. Paragraph 12 of the complaint alleges that Folsom Ventures, LLC and Lennar are alter egos and there is a factual issue regarding who is bound by the CC&Rs.

Defendants argue that the breach of express warranty claim does not highlight any specific warranty provision. Plaintiffs sufficiently plead the terms of the contracts according to their legal intendment and effect. To plead a contract claim according to its legal effect, the plaintiff should allege the making and then proceed to allege the substance of its relevant terms. (See McKell v. Washington Mut. Inc. (2006) 142 Cal.App.4th 1457, 1489; Pneucrete Corp. v. United States Fidelity & Guaranty Co. (1935) 7 Cal.App.2d 733, 741-742.) Plaintiffs have done so in this case.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
